description: Payment flow abuse — Stripe / Square / PayPal / Adyen / Braintree integrations consistently yield $5k+ bounties. Common classes: payment_intent ID confusion, capture/refund races, webhook secret rotation, 3DS setup_intent reuse, idempotency-key per-customer scope, currency confusion, tax-zero exploits.
craft_guidance: 1. MAP THE FLOW: identify create_intent -> confirm -> capture -> webhook -> capture_settled. Each step is a race / replay / confusion target.
2. PROCESSOR FINGERPRINT: pi_/seti_/ch_ prefixes = Stripe; PAY-/EC- = PayPal; tok_ = Stripe legacy. Different processors have different idempotency / webhook semantics.
3. INTENT ID CROSS-ACCOUNT: capture intent A on attacker account and try to attribute it to victim's order. Server-side checks must verify (intent.customer == request.user).
4. AMOUNT MUTATION POST-CONFIRM: confirm $1, capture $1, but order metadata says $1000. Server-side capture/order amount mismatch = free order.
5. REFUND RACE: simultaneous refund + capture on same intent. Some apps debit account TWICE (once per call) before reconciling.
6. WEBHOOK SECRET ROTATION: Stripe allows two simultaneous secrets during rotation; both validate for ~24h. Old captured event + old secret = replayable into new system.

7. SETUP_INTENT REUSE: setup_intent (saving card for future) doesn't go through 3DS for subsequent uses. Capture victim's setup_intent (via OAuth scope or session leak), confirm new charge against it.
8. IDEMPOTENCY-KEY SCOPE: Stripe idempotency-key MUST be scoped to (account, key). Per-key scoping = same key, different account = duplicate-charge.
9. CURRENCY CONFUSION: order in USD, charge in IDR. 1 USD = 15000 IDR. Server stores 'price=1' regardless of currency = pay 1 IDR.
10. TAX/SHIPPING ZERO: app validates subtotal but not total. Set tax=-100 / shipping=-100 / discount=999 in update_intent calls.

intent_id_cross_account.description: Reuse a payment_intent ID created by attacker on a victim's order. Server-side: order.charge = stripe.PaymentIntent.retrieve(client_provided_id). If app trusts the ID without verifying customer/amount/account, attacker pays $1 for a $1000 order.

probes.description: Attach attacker's $1 payment_intent to victim's $1000 order. If accepted (200 + order_paid), no server-side amount/customer verification.

capture_refund_race.description: Capture and refund the SAME intent simultaneously. Some apps credit refund to the user's balance AND complete the order, netting attacker the goods + refunded money.

probes.description: Fire concurrent capture + refund (use test_race_condition). If both succeed and balance + order_paid both update, race confirmed.

webhook_secret_rotation_race.description: During Stripe webhook secret rotation, BOTH old and new secrets validate for the rotation window (~24h). Captured webhook + old secret = replayable as legitimate.

probes.description: Replay a captured webhook signed with previous secret. If accepted within rotation window, attacker can manufacture events.

setup_intent_reuse_no_3ds.description: setup_intent saves a card for future charges WITHOUT 3DS challenge. Attacker who captures victim's setup_intent (via OAuth scope leak or browser-extension exfil) charges victim's card without SCA.

probes.description: Charge using victim's setup_intent. 3DS not required; if 200, attacker bypassed SCA.

idempotency_key_cross_customer.description: Idempotency-Key MUST be scoped to (account, key, body-hash). Some integrations scope only to (key) — same key with different customer ID = duplicate charge attributed to attacker.

probes.description: Send same idempotency-key with attacker customer first, then victim customer. If second returns first's response (attacker charged), scoping is broken.

currency_confusion.description: Order created in USD; charge created in low-value currency (IDR, VND). Server stores numeric price regardless of currency. 1 unit pays for 1 USD worth of goods.

probes.description: Charge IDR amount on a USD order. If accepted and order_paid=true, currency normalization is broken.

tax_shipping_negative.description: App validates subtotal range (positive, < $X) but not tax / shipping / discount fields. Negative values flip the total.

probes.description: Negative tax. If checkout accepts, total drops to $1 from $100.
Discount exceeds subtotal — negative total. Some apps refund the difference.
